codeReview.summary: The fix does clean the success-path artifact: the reviewed 50c09d7 debug-emulator artifact shows `recovery-phrase.xml` with 18 `[redacted]` cells, the `Back up your recovery phrase` title preserved, wallet evidence still present in `main-wallet.xml` / `after-relaunch.xml`, and no other named dump redacted. But the implementation only sanitizes dumps whose filename starts with `recovery-phrase`, so RecoveryPhrase-screen failure paths can still upload raw mnemonic-bearing XML via `window_dump.xml` / `flow-error.xml` before the script reaches `main-wallet`.

issues.description: Sanitization is keyed only off `name.startswith("recovery-phrase")`. During the same RecoveryPhrase stage, `scroll_into_view()` calls bare `dump_ui()` (line 358) and `_dump_flow_error()` writes `dump_ui("flow-error")` (line 1578). Those calls rewrite `/tmp/emulator-ui/window_dump.xml` or `flow-error.xml` with the raw RecoveryPhrase hierarchy if scrolling/tapping fails before the next non-sensitive dump, so CI failure artifacts can still leak mnemonic words. The fix needs content-based RecoveryPhrase detection (as the feature contract requested) or equivalent sanitization for every dump captured while that screen is on top.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `ci-runner` skill still teaches secret-leak scans that only cover `logcat-*.txt` for mnemonic and long-hex checks, even though the mission now relies on XML dump sanitization to prevent RecoveryPhrase leaks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/skills/ci-runner/SKILL.md:119-126` scopes mnemonic/hex scans to `/tmp/ci-artifacts/logcat-*.txt`, while the new guidance in `.factory/library/user-testing.md:35-58` says validators must treat uploaded `*.xml` dumps as sensitive and verify the RecoveryPhrase dump is scrubbed.

summary: Reviewed worker session `1204b01e-7a50-4bf7-8c01-a40a45f2ea6b`, the prior failing review, and both relevant diffs (`efd5a50` and `50c09d7`). The green 50c09d7 artifact proves the named `recovery-phrase.xml` is redacted and milestone evidence remains intact, but the dump helper still leaks the RecoveryPhrase hierarchy through unnamed/error dumps on failure paths because sanitization only keys off the filename. Marked fail until RecoveryPhrase detection/sanitization covers those dump paths too.
